page: Microsoft Azure DS13_v2 is a DSv2-family virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz. The system features 56.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 115 GB of local storage and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The processor architecture supports AVX and AVX-512 instruction sets, alongside AES cryptography acceleration, but lacks AVX-512 VNNI and AMX matrix extensions.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance divergence between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression tasks. Conversely, multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is consistently average, with cached small-block reads reaching 720.03 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 76.69 GB/sec.

With 56.0 GB of RAM relative to 8 vCPUs, this server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7:1, positioning it as a memory-dense resource. This profile is suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving, where it achieves average benchmark throughput. However, the weak single-core performance presents a tradeoff for compute-bound or single-threaded tasks. It supports small-model LLM inference at average speeds, but medium-model inference drops to the bottom 25%, and it lacks GPU acceleration for larger AI workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure DS13_v2 is a DSv2-family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 56.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB per core, and includes 115 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance exhibits a performance profile characterized by weak single-core CPU execution but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Specifically, multi-core benchmarks, Passmark memory tests, and Redis SET operations fall within the middle 50% tier. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this server suitable for memory-dense workloads such as database operations, caching, and static web serving, though its weak single-threaded performance and lack of GPU acceleration limit its utility for intensive compute or large-scale AI inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS13_v2 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 56 GB of RAM, and 115 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and 115 GB of local storage for database and caching workloads.
